params.text:
The environment matters

Surroundings have a powerful effect on how a person feels and copes. A confusing or overwhelming space can add to distress, while a calm, familiar one can settle it. Small adjustments often make a real difference.

  • Reduce noise and clutter: too much noise or busyness can overload and unsettle a person.
  • Good lighting: bright, even light reduces shadows that can be misread or frightening.
  • Familiar cues: clear signs, familiar objects and personal belongings help with orientation and comfort.
  • Safe to move: space to walk freely supports independence and eases restlessness.

Meaningful activity

Everyone needs purpose and enjoyment. Meaningful activity — occupation that connects with who a person is — supports wellbeing, reduces boredom and can lessen distress. It does not have to be organised or elaborate.

  • Match interests and abilities: build on hobbies, past work and things the person still enjoys and can do.
  • Focus on the doing, not the result: the pleasure of folding towels or sorting buttons matters more than a perfect outcome.
  • Use the senses: music, textures, scents, gardening and gentle movement can all engage and soothe.
  • Keep it dignified: activities should feel adult and purposeful, never childish.

Boredom, loneliness and a lack of purpose are common causes of distress. By shaping a calm environment and offering meaningful occupation, you support wellbeing and often prevent difficulties before they begin.

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the correct gap to complete these points about environment and activity.
params.textField: A calm environment with less *noise* and good *lighting* can ease distress. Offering meaningful *activity* that matches a person's interests supports wellbeing, because boredom and *loneliness* are common causes of distress.
